meaning: v.发表社论,插入编者意见
examples: He couldn't resist the opportunity, when reporting on a burglary, to editorialize on security systems.
Well your employees have the same reaction when you editorialize, and whine about decisions that are made.
And I'm happy to have human events editorialize for extending the very positive aspects of jobs saved and created as part of the recovery funding.
Regrettably, Collison cannot resist the temptation to editorialize about Indonesian politics and sometimes seems to lose the thread of the narrative as he pursues various sidetracks.
